opportunities.title: Same Bug, Second Payday: The Verus-Ethereum Bridge Got Drained Twice by the Same Flaw
opportunities.why_now: Blockaid confirmed the July 23 $7.5M Verus-Ethereum drain reused the exact exploit path from May, and it landed within hours of the $24M AFX Trade bridge hack — two bridges, one lesson, one news cycle.
opportunities.angle: Incident autopsy comparing the May and July Verus exploits side-by-side, arguing 'known vulnerability' is now a standing invitation, not an accident

opportunities.title: Your Ledger Was the Attack Surface: Zilliqa App Bug Let Hackers Recover Private Keys
opportunities.why_now: A hardware wallet vulnerability that recovers signer keys is the nightmare scenario for the 'not your keys, not your coins' crowd, and it's fresh as of July 22.
opportunities.angle: Explainer on how the vuln worked + practical checklist for hardware wallet users on firmware/app hygiene, with 'this is why cold storage isn't magic' framing

opportunities.title: $31.6M in Bridges, One 7-Hour Window: Bridges Remain Crypto's Weakest Link in 2026
opportunities.why_now: Two separate bridge exploits (AFX $24M, Verus $7.5M) hit within the same day, echoing the exact bridge-security thesis this channel has been calling since 2022 — the pattern hasn't changed, only the zeros.
opportunities.angle: Data-led roundup: tally 2026 bridge losses YTD, chart repeat offenders, close with 'if it bridges, it bleeds' thesis

opportunities.title: Wrench Attacks Are Up 12x — Your Threat Model Needs to Include Physical Violence
opportunities.why_now: CertiK's $124M/6-month figure on wrench attacks is a stark, citable stat that reframes crypto security beyond code audits into personal safety, timely as self-custody narratives peak.
opportunities.angle: Service piece: what a wrench attack actually looks like, why whales/self-custody advocates are targets, concrete opsec steps (decoy wallets, duress PINs, doxxing hygiene)

opportunities.title: Audits Didn't Save SecondFi or SummerFi — So What Will Institutions Trust Instead?
opportunities.why_now: Hacken's report on trust signals beyond audits landed the same week two protocols (SecondFi, SummerFi) shut down citing exploits despite prior audits — a perfect real-time case study for the report's thesis.
opportunities.angle: Trend piece tying Hacken's findings directly to the SecondFi/SummerFi shutdowns as proof audits are necessary-but-not-sufficient, propose what 'beyond audit' trust actually means
